macro.title: Global luxury housing markets move toward equilibrium in 2026
macro.what: Christie’s International Real Estate debuted its Prime Sentiment Index, 2026 scored 14.4 vs 15.6 in 2025 as markets normalized.
macro.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
macro.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

macro.title: Redfin Economists’ Weekly Take: Rates Will Jump Around As Markets Weigh Iran War and Fed Outlook
macro.what: This Week In A Nutshell: We’re expecting rates to continue bouncing around this week, as the Middle East conflict continues into its third week with no clear off-ramp and markets anticipate the upcoming Fed meeting
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: The Oldest Americans Held More Real Estate Wealth Than Ever Before In 2025
macro.what: For the first time, the oldest Americans (70+) held a larger share of real estate wealth than middle-aged Americans (40-54)
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Mortgage Rates Jump to Six-Month High as Inflation Fears, Iran War Rattle Markets
macro.what: Today’s rate increase is due almost entirely to inflation jitters
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: ICE’s Matt Dowd on targeting mortgage servicing call volume with AI agents
housing.what: ICE launched AI voice and chat agents to answer routine servicing questions 24/7, reduce call-center volume and route complex cases to staff.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Qualia CEO Nate Baker: “AI is as significant as language and fire”
housing.what: Qualia CEO Nate Baker sat down with HousingWire to discuss updates to his company's agentic AI platform and timelines for industry evolution.
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Mortgage demand plummets 11% as 30-year fixed rate rises to 6.3%
housing.what: MBA dats shows mortgage applications fell 10.9% as the 30-year fixed rate hit 6.3%, while the purchase index rose 1% week over week.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: The 3% rule: Why this pricing move is driving faster home sales
housing.what: HousingWire data shows 3% price cuts are working best in mid-market housing, where $350K to $650K homes are posting the strongest absorption.
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.title: eXp Realty adds coming soon syndication to Realtor.com, Homes.com, ComeHome.com
housing.what: eXp Realty will syndicate coming soon listings via Zenlist to Realtor.com, Homes.com and ComeHome.com starting April 15.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: California transit agency land could support 240,000 homes
housing.what: California lawmakers have spent years passing bills aimed at increasing housing supply, with an emphasis on building more homes near transit
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Letting Home Sellers Test the Waters Before Listing Could Boost Housing Supply as Much as 12%
housing.what: Redfin estimates inventory could increase 6%-12% in markets where sellers have the flexibility to test out pricing strategies before listing
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Methodology: Inventory Impact of Phased Marketing
housing.what: This methodology accompanies Redfin’s report about how letting home sellers test the market before officially listing could boost housing supply
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.
